Comment On: CFPB-2023-0038
Date:
Monday, July 10, 2023 3:20:39 PM
CAUTION: This email originated from a non-government domain. DO NOT click links or open attachments unless
you recognize and/or trust the sender. Contact Cybersecurity Incident Response Team (CSIRT) at 202-435-7200 or
report a suspicious email.
Hello, 
Regarding  CFPB-2023-0038, I only have experience with a medical payment product for my
pets. I use Care Credit and I put my vet bills on it. I otherwise would not be able to afford
veterinary care for my pets. I could put the costs on my other credit card, but Care Credit
allows me 6 months to pay off the amount without accruing interest. 
I see how this product would hurt many other people. If you don't pay off the balance in 6
months then all the interest from the 6 months is tacked onto your balance and then interest is
charged on the total amount. So if you aren't careful this card can get you into trouble. 
My opinion is that if you take away products like this, then you need to help people in other
ways. My husband has medical debt from before he met me. He was on a payment plan with
the doctor's office. After a while, they sent his unpaid balance to a debt collector even after he
made regular payments as per the payment agreement with the doctor's office. Medical
facilities shouldn't be allowed to do this if you are making payments on time. This debt has
affected his credit score and our potential to buy a house. 
In short, the medical care system in the USA needs rehauling entirely. You aren't going to fix
things by taking away a credit product that helps some people get the care they need for our
crazy system. It would be better to regulate the credit products and what they can do rather
than eliminating them entirely. 
I hope this helps, 
Regina Cleaves
